item.title: Introducing Claude for Small Business
item.description: We're launching Claude for Small Business, a package of connectors and ready-to-run workflows that put Claude inside the tools small businesses use every day.

item.title: Scorched Earth 2000 HTML Port
item.description: Debug Console

item.title: Arrival of the Personal Cluster
item.description: BACK TO BLOG

item.title: The Per-Minute Calling Myth: The Hidden Economics of Voice and Video Calling APIs
item.description: Why do most communication platforms charge per-minute fees even when peer-to-peer calls never traverse their infrastructure? A technical look at how modern WebRTC systems actually work, and why per-minute billing for internet communication is increasingly difficult to justify — technically and ethically.

item.title: Adopting Helix-isms | Kris Tun
item.description: An exploration of adopting Helix-isms into my Neovim workflow

item.title: I Don't Even Know What To Build Anymore - Vance Lucas
item.description: UIs with buttons and charts are not cool anymore. All that matters is AI agents now. APIs, CLIs and MCPs. It's all about the data, bro. Where is your MCP? Or so the discourse goes...

item.title: The hardware isn't broken, the documentation is !
item.description: Silicon erratas or how my first analog ASIC is dead before arrival.

item.title: The harness that lied — Are You There?
item.description: On the verdict-before-evidence pattern in security research — and how I caught my own PoC confirming itself regardless of the facts.

item.title: Star Color and the Evolution of Space on the Screen
item.description: How Project Hail Mary's faithful depiction of space changes science fiction forever.

item.title: You need to calm down
item.description: AI isn’t changing the fundamentals of building software. It’s changing the speed. Don’t confuse the two.
item.author: (https://petro.blog)

item.title: Building for the joy of building | jola.dev
item.description: My path into programming and why I've been obsessed for 20 years.
item.author: (https://jola.dev/about)

item.title: How LLMs Really Work
item.description: If you have used ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude, you have already formed an intuition about what these systems do. You type something in, and text comes back that feels coherent, knowledgeable, and sometimes eerily human. But the machinery underneath is simultaneously simpler and stranger than most people expect.

item.title: Removing the Modem and GPS from my 2024 RAV4 Hybrid
item.description: Modern cars are computers on wheels that send home nonstop telemetry about you. In this post I remove my 2024 RAV4 Hybrid's modem and GPS to prevent that :)

item.title: I Read OpenSSL for Fun and Found a Nonce Leak
item.description: I was poking around the OpenSSL source code recently. Not really hunting for anything specific (one of the most heavily audited codebases), just curious about how the new post-quantum crypto stuff was wired up in version 4.0.0. I went in expecting to find nothing interesting. Instead I tripped over a single-character logic bug that leaks cryptographic randomness onto the stack on every signing call. Quick disclaimer: I am not a crypto person.

item.title: EventQL: A SQL-Inspired Query Language Designed for Event Sourcing
item.description: EventQL: A SQL-Inspired Query Language Designed for Event Sourcing

item.title: Rubenerd: Fractions in HTML
item.description: Expressing 1/3 with ⅓, and related adventures.
item.author: (Ruben Schade)

item.title: Was my $48K GPU server worth it?
item.description: In 2024 I quit my FAANG job to become an independent researcher. To do this I needed GPUs, so I built “grumbl”, a 6x 6000 Ada GPU server. This blog describes the build, some of the issu…

item.title: On Planet Quantos, Programmers Flee
item.description: An interactive story about porting a classical rank-select function to a reversible (quantum-oracle-style) form. Step through five C implementations, each adding one constraint from the world of unitary computation.
item.author: (Nishant Shukla)

item.title: The Wide Paved Road versus the Narrow Footpath - Peter's Path
item.description: Explore the wide paved road of ease leading to ruin versus the narrow footpath of hardship leading to redemption across stories, myths, and faiths.
item.author: (Peter)

item.title: I was asked to install malware during a fake interview
item.description: My experience with Contagious Interviews (Malware delivered through fake developer job interviews)

item.title: Slowing Down My Coding Agents to Get More Done – Robert W
item.description: Your backlog is sitting idle waiting to get worked on, and it could be showing up pre-baked, ready for verification.

item.title: Prayer-Driven Development
item.description: The term “Prayer-Driven Development” was coined by Davor Pavleković for deployments where hope is your only strategy. That was before AI agents made it an industry standard. Everyone Did Their Part. Nobody Saw the Whole. You’ve been here. The prompt that needs “MANDATORY” in all caps. The instruction that says “DO NOT SKIP” because the model skipped it three times already. The system prompt that reads like a legal contract because you can’t trust the other party.

item.title: Taking control of the SQLite WAL – Philip O'Toole
item.description: rqlite is a lightweight, open-source, fault-tolerant relational database built on SQLite and Raft. Version 10 is out now. rqlite has a particular relationship with the SQLite Write-Ahead Log (WAL). SQLite, left to itself, manages its own WAL: it checkpoints when the WAL grows, it checkpoints when the last connection closes, and it decides when frames move from…

item.title: Software Engineers are Obsolete
item.description: In my first interview for a developer position, I shared a link to my personal project with the interviewer. It was a website for learning how to program. I created it from the ground up. I built the

item.title: 5 Years and $5M Later: Inventing a New Programming Language for Web Development Was a Mistake | Wasp
item.description: After 5 years and $5M building Wasp, we're replacing our custom config language with TypeScript. Here's why we thought a new language was a good idea, what we learned, and why TypeScript won.
item.author: (https://github.com/matijasos)

item.title: Results Age
Setting up a free *.city.state.us locality domain
item.description: In the US, you can get a domain name like `somename.city.state.us` for free. If your town has its own domain, you can get nameservers from Amazon Lightsail, send the *Interim .US Domain Template* to the delegated manager for your locality, then point DNS entries at your webhost.

item.title: Why I'm leaving GitHub for Forgejo | Jorijn Schrijvershof
item.description: I left GitHub for self-hosted Forgejo on a hardened NUC. The reason is digital sovereignty, not reliability outages. Here's the thinking and the architecture.

item.title: Best Practices in .NET I Don't Like Anymore
item.description:
Online Workshop: Vibe Coding for Production: https://dometrain.com/workshop/vibe-coding-for-production/?ref=nick-chapsas&promo=mail-list&coupon_code=3YEARS
Get every Dometrain 40% off with code 3YEARS: https://dometrain.com/courses/?ref=nick-chapsas&promo=youtube&coupon_code=3YEARS
Hello, everybody. I'm Nick, and in this video, I want to talk about previous .NET practices that I used to consider great, but I no longer use for one reason or another. In this video, I will explain why.
Don't forget to comment, like and subscribe :)
Social Media: Follow me on GitHub: https://github.com/Elfocrash Follow me on Twitter: https://twitter.com/nickchapsas Connect on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nick-chapsas
#dotnet #csharp
item.author: (Nick Chapsas)

item.title: SpaceX
item.description: SpaceX designs, manufactures and launches advanced rockets and spacecraft.
